'''Joe Batchelor''' (born 28 October 1994) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

He has played for the [[Coventry Bears]] and the [[York City Knights]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]]. Batchelor has spent time on loan from [[St Helens R.F.C.|Saints]] at [[York City Knights|York]] and the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[2019 RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]]. From 2026 he will play for [[Hull F.C.]] after signing a three-year deal with the club.

==Background==
Born in [[Wakefield]], [[West Yorkshire, England]], Batchelor played junior [[rugby league]] with [[Dewsbury Celtic]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Joe Batchelor & Dougie Foster - England Selection |url=https://www.pitchero.com/clubs/dewsburycelticrlfc/news/joe-batchelor-dougie-foster-england-selection-604607.html |publisher=Pitch Hero |access-date=10 October 2022 |date=20 June 2012}}</ref> 

He is the brother of [[James Batchelor (rugby league)|James Batchelor]] who plays for [[Hull KR]] in the [[Super League]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
Batchelor started his career as an academy player at [[Sheffield Eagles]]. In 2015, he spent a year in [[Australia]], where he played for [[Bathurst St Patrick's]] in the [[Group 10 Rugby League]] competition.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Whitelaw |first1=Anya |title=Former St Pat's lock Joe Batchelor is set to represent England on the world stage |url=https://www.westernadvocate.com.au/story/7925860/from-st-pats-to-englands-world-cup-squad/ |website=Western Advocate |access-date=10 October 2022 |date=1 October 2022}}</ref> He returned to the Eagles in 2016, but the club's reserve team was discontinued shortly afterwards.<ref name="Batchelor faces make or break year">{{cite web |title=Batchelor faces make or break year |url=https://www.totalrl.com/batchelor-faces-make-or-break-year/ |website=Total Rugby League |access-date=10 October 2022 |date=28 February 2021}}</ref> After contacting [[Coventry Bears]] coach Tom Tsang, he agreed to join the club, and made his professional debut in May 2016 against Doncaster.<ref>{{cite web |title=Coventry Bears 20 Doncaster RL 32: Improving Bears give Donny a real scare |url=https://www.coventrytelegraph.net/sport/other-sport/rugby-league/coventry-bears-20-doncaster-rl-11337448 |website=Coventry Telegraph |access-date=10 October 2022 |date=16 May 2016}}</ref>

===York City Knights===
In December 2016, [[York City Knights]] announced that they had signed Batchelor.<ref>{{cite web |title=Bruiser back in the ranks: Halifax packman Adam Robinson signs for York City Knights |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/14982245.bruiser-back-in-the-ranks-halifax-packman-adam-robinson-signs-for-york-city-knights/ |website=York Press |access-date=10 October 2022 |date=22 December 2016}}</ref> He debuted for the club in a [[Challenge Cup]] match against [[Egremont Rangers]], scoring a try in a 48–8 win.<ref>{{cite web |title=Challenge Cup reaction: York City Knights boss hails improvement - and discipline - in the face of Egremont fouls and feistiness |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/15118964.challenge-cup-reaction-york-city-knights-boss-hails-improvement-and-discipline-in-the-face-of-egremont-fouls-and-feistiness/ |website=York Press |access-date=10 October 2022 |date=26 February 2017}}</ref> He scored 15 tries in 21 appearances in his first season at the club, and was named as the ''[[York Press]]'' Player of the Year for 2017.<ref>{{cite web |title=Knights Player of the Year final standings |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/15624050.knights-player-of-the-year-final-standings/ |website=York Press |access-date=10 October 2022 |date=27 October 2017}}</ref> Despite attracting interest from other clubs, he agreed to sign a new contract with York for the following season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Second-row star Joe Batchelor rejects rivals to agree new deal with York City Knights |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/15576494.second-row-star-joe-batchelor-rejects-rivals-to-agree-new-deal-with-york-city-knights/ |website=York Press |access-date=10 October 2022 |date=5 October 2017}}</ref> In the 2018 season, he was the club's top try scorer with 23 tries in 27 games, helping the club win promotion to the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Former Knights man Batchelor called up to England |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/20206654.former-knights-man-batchelor-called-england/ |website=York Press |access-date=10 October 2022 |date=13 June 2022}}</ref>

===St Helens===
In May 2018, Batchelor agreed to join [[Super League]] side [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], signing a three-year contract with the club ahead of the 2019 season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Saints attract Batchelor |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/52392/saints-attract-batchelor |publisher=The Rugby Football League |access-date=10 October 2022 |date=14 May 2018}}</ref> During his first season at the club, he returned to York City Knights on loan.<ref>{{cite web |title=Joe Batchelor returns to York City Knights on month loan |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/17417045.joe-batchelor-returns-york-city-knights-month-loan/ |website=York Press |access-date=10 October 2022 |date=7 February 2019}}</ref> The initial one-month loan was extended for the whole season,<ref>{{cite web |title=St Helens' Joe Batchelor extends loan spell at York City Knights |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/17475576.st-helens-joe-batchelor-extends-loan-spell-york-city-knights/ |website=York Press |access-date=10 October 2022 |date=4 March 2019}}</ref> but Saints exercised the option to recall him. He made his Super League debut for St Helens against [[Hull F.C.|Hull]] in April 2019.<ref name="Joe Batchelor agrees new St Helens deal">{{cite web |title=Joe Batchelor agrees new St Helens deal |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/35920/joe-batchelor-agrees-new-st-helens-deal |publisher=The Rugby Football League |access-date=10 October 2022 |date=24 August 2021}}</ref> Later in the season, he also played for [[Leigh Centurions]] on a [[Rugby league dual registration|dual registration]] deal. Batchelor's first team opportunities at St Helens were limited, and he made only eight appearances during his first two seasons at the club.<ref name="Batchelor faces make or break year" />

His breakthrough came in the 2021 season, becoming a regular starter in the second-row for the club following the departures of [[Zeb Taia]] and [[Dom Peyroux]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Catalans Dragons v St Helens – Patient Joe Batchelor rewarded for adhering to strong work ethic |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/catalans-dragons-v-st-helens-patient-joe-batchelor-rewarded-for-adhering-to-strong-work-ethic-3412792 |website=Yorkshire Post |access-date=10 October 2022 |date=8 October 2021}}</ref> On 17 July 2021, he played for St Helens in their 26–12 win against [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-celebrate-challenge-cup-21081897/|title=How St Helens players have celebrated their first Challenge Cup win in 13 years|website=www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk|access-date=18 July 2021|archive-date=18 July 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210718102540/https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-celebrate-challenge-cup-21081897|url-status=dead}}</ref> In August 2021, Batchelor extended his contract with Saints until the end of the 2023 season.<ref name="Joe Batchelor agrees new St Helens deal" /> On 9 October 2021, he played for St Helens in their 12–10 victory over [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[2021 Super League Grand Final]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
On 24 September 2022, Batchelor played for St Helens in their [[2022 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos]],<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/sep/24/st-helens-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=St Helens sink Leeds to win record fourth consecutive Grand Final|newspaper=The Observer|date=24 September 2022 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref> and was rewarded with a further contract extension until 2025.<ref>{{cite web |title=Joe Batchelor: St Helens and England forward signs new three-year contract |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63243445 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=21 March 2024 |date=13 October 2022}}</ref> He played 15 games for St Helens in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished third on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/23856044.paul-wellens-q-saints-review-year-look-2024/|title=Paul Wellens Q&A: Saints review of year and a look to 2024|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk|date=14 October 2023 }}</ref>
Batchelor played 17 matches for St Helens in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish sixth on the table. Batchelor played in St Helens golden point extra-time playoff loss against Warrington.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2024/sep/28/warrington-st-helens-super-league-eliminator-playoff-rugby-league-match-report/|title=
‘Soul destroying’: Warrington eliminate St Helens in golden drop goal thriller|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===Hull FC===
On 16 September 2025, it was announced that Batchelor would be departing St Helens at the end of the 2025 Super League season. On the same day, it was revealed he had signed a contract to join [[Hull F.C.]] starting in 2026.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cp8w5xk98nwo/|title=Hull FC sign Batchelor and Bell from St Helens|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===England===
Batchelor was one of five St Helens players selected by [[England national rugby league team|England]] in the squad for the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Joe Batchelor one of five St Helens players in England World Cup squad |url=https://www.liverpoolworld.uk/sport/joe-batchelor-one-of-five-st-helens-players-in-england-world-cup-squad-3862700 |website=Liverpool World |access-date=10 October 2022 |date=30 September 2022}}</ref>
